title: 5 relation/upsert ops omit the --dry-run their orchestrator-script siblings offer (dual-surface optional-param asymmetry)
description: The op-coverage parity gate (FGAP-010, landed dc7f514) reports, non-fatally, that 5 ops — append-relation, remove-relation, replace-relation, append-relations, upsert-block-item — do not declare a 'dryRun' parameter in their typebox parameters schema, while their orchestrator-script siblings under scripts/orchestrator/ DO parse a '--dry-run' flag. So a no-write preview is reachable out-of-pi via the script but not in-pi via the op or the CLI. This is distinct from amend-config / write-schema / promote-item, whose op schemas DO declare dryRun (those were the prior detector's false-positives, now corrected). Secondary, separate concern tracked here: the parity-check divergence detector itself carries two dormant edges the re-audit flagged — (a) opDeclaresParam reads only top-level schema .properties, so an op expressed via allOf/$ref/nested would yield a false divergence; (b) scriptParsesFlag matches a quote-hugged flag literal anywhere in the script text, so a print-only literal (a flag named in help text but not parsed) would false-match. Neither triggers on the current tree (all ops use flat Type.Object; no script prints a bare flag literal).

evidence.reference: parses --dry-run; the append-relation op schema declares ordinal but not dryRun
parses --dry-run; the remove-relation op schema declares no optional flags
parses --dry-run; the replace-relation op schema declares ordinal but not dryRun
parses --dry-run; the append-relations op schema declares no top-level dryRun
parses --dry-run; the upsert-block-item op schema declares idField but not dryRun

impact: Dry-run preview asymmetry between the in-pi op / CLI surface and the out-of-pi orchestrator scripts for edge mutation and atomic upsert: an in-pi caller cannot preview these five mutations the way the scripts can. Minor and advisory rather than a correctness defect, but it is exactly the dual-surface drift the parity gate exists to surface, so leaving it unaddressed lets the surfaces diverge further.
proposed_resolution: CLOSED across TASK-010 (8fcf02d, the 4 relation ops) + TASK-011 (4b72b86, upsert-block-item). The 5 relation/upsert ops now share ONE dryRun preview with their orchestrator scripts (the byRef/upsert library fns gained opts?:{dryRun} that validate the prospective + return without writing; ops declare/thread dryRun; scripts delegate). The parity-check gate's dual-surface divergence report is now at ZERO. Also tracked from this arc: FGAP-016 (pre-existing truncateHead measurement nuance, P3, non-leak).
